full_prompt: Design a wearable single-joint knee exoskeleton assist cuff targeting stair-climbing assistance in community-dwelling older adults. The device consists of a thigh cuff, a shank cuff, and a single actuated hinge joint crossing the knee with a custom brushless DC actuator + 20:1 harmonic drive delivering a peak assistive moment of 30 N-m and a continuous moment of 15 N-m at the knee; shaft RPM peaks at 120 rpm (2 Hz). The cuffs are carbon-fiber-reinforced polymer shells (CFRP quasi-isotropic, E 45 GPa, tensile strength 500 MPa) with foam liners, sized for a 50th percentile adult with thigh circumference 480 mm and shank circumference 380 mm; total device mass must not exceed 1.8 kg including a 48 Wh LiFePO4 battery. The device must simultaneously satisfy: (R1) max von Mises stress in the CFRP cuff shells under the 30 N-m peak assistive moment plus 1.5 g user lateral maneuver must not exceed 250 MPa (tensile 500 / SF 2.0), (R2) contact pressure between the CFRP shell and skin through the foam liner must not exceed 16 kPa average under normal donning to avoid pressure ulcers per ISO 10536-2, (R3) peak linear acceleration on the CFRP shell at the 2 Hz actuator peak must not cause a first-mode resonance; the first natural frequency of the assembled cuff with actuator must be at least 10 Hz (5x operating frequency), (R4) battery endurance at continuous 15 N-m, 60 rpm duty must be at least 2 hours on the 48 Wh battery (electrical + mechanical efficiency must exceed 0.70), and (R5) total device mass including actuator + battery must not exceed 1.8 kg. Vibration analysis is required for R3. Thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver a STEP AP242 assembly with thigh cuff, shank cuff, hinge, actuator housing, and battery compartment as sub-bodies plus a verification report listing max von Mises, shell-skin pressure, first natural frequency, battery endurance, and total mass.
short_prompt:
Design a wearable single-joint knee exoskeleton assist cuff for stair-climbing assistance in community-dwelling older adults. The device has a thigh cuff and a shank cuff (CFRP shells with foam liner, sized for 50th-percentile adult with 480 mm thigh and 380 mm shank circumference) joined by a single actuated knee hinge driven by a BLDC + 20:1 harmonic drive delivering 30 N-m peak / 15 N-m continuous at up to 120 rpm (2 Hz), powered by a 48 Wh LiFePO4 battery.

Design to ISO 10536-2-style pressure-ulcer prevention guidance for the skin-contact limit. Cuffs are clamped around the limb by tensioned straps; the hinge is a single-DOF revolute joint.

Load cases.
- LC1 peak moment + lateral: 30 N-m actuator peak at the knee plus 1.5 g user lateral maneuver.
- LC2 donning contact: distributed pressure at the shell-skin interface.
- LC3 modal: first natural frequency of the assembled cuff with actuator, relative to 2 Hz forcing.
- LC4 endurance: continuous 15 N-m at 60 rpm on 48 Wh battery.

Total device mass (with actuator and battery) is a wearable-comfort constraint.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Assistive knee moment curve during stair ascent is read from published biomechanics (Riener, Perry), not FEA.

prompt.title: Single-joint knee exoskeleton assist cuff
prompt.brief: Design a 1.8 kg CFRP knee exoskeleton with 30 N-m peak / 15 N-m continuous assist, 2 Hz actuator, 48 Wh battery for 2 h endurance on stair ascent, passing shell stress, skin pressure, resonance, and mass limits.

material.cuff_shell: CFRP quasi-isotropic, E 45 GPa, tensile 500 MPa

boundary_conditions.cuffs: clamped around limb via tensioned strap
boundary_conditions.hinge: single DOF revolute joint at knee

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: shell for cuffs with composite layup

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Actuator thermal management is outside structural scope.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Cuff shells are stiffness-limited not buckling-limited.

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: Full human factors (IEC 62366-1) with older adults
